title: ServerSignedAuthClaims
description: Canonical body serialised via OSPP Canonical Form (spec/06-security.md §4.8) and base64-encoded into the `signedAuthorization.data` field of a ServerSignedAuth message for ECDSA P-256 signing. Per profiles/offline/ble-handshake.md §4.2.1. 12 required claims — no optional fields. `durationSeconds` (finding N3) is the authorized duration the station clamps the session to (profiles/offline/ble-session.md §3/§6); `creditsAuthorized` is the issue-time pre-debit basis, NOT a settlement cap — the server recomputes final billing per the Billing Authority (04-flows §6 / reconciliation §8.2), so the settled cost MAY exceed it. Both are signed so the station enforces a server-authorized duration it can cryptographically trust, not an app-supplied request. The digest signed by the server's private key is SHA-256 over the canonical bytes directly (NOT over the base64 form).

authId.description: Server-side unique identifier for this authorisation. Mirrors the `passId` / `offlineTxId` convention (lowercase prefix, lowercase hex).

sub.description: User subject identifier the authorisation is issued to.

deviceId.description: Mobile device the authorisation is bound to. The station MUST cross-check against `Hello.deviceId` (§4.2.2 check #4).

sessionId.description: Session this authorisation activates. The station MUST cross-check against the outer envelope's `sessionId` (§4.2.2 check #5).

stationId.description: Station this authorisation is valid for. The station MUST cross-check against its own provisioned `stationId` (§4.2.2 check #3) — prevents cross-station replay.

durationSeconds.description: Authorized service duration in seconds (finding N3). The station MUST clamp requestedDurationSeconds to this signed value (profiles/offline/ble-session.md §6). Signed so the station enforces a server-authorized bound, not an app-supplied request.

creditsAuthorized.description: Authorized credit budget for this session (finding N3) — sets the issue-time pre-debit. NOT a settlement cap: the server recomputes final billing per the Billing Authority (04-flows §6), so the settled cost MAY exceed it (reconciliation §8.2). Signed.

appNonce.description: Base64-encoded 32-byte cryptographically random nonce, equal to the `appNonce` the app writes in the upcoming `Hello` message. The server MUST read this from the `POST /sessions/offline-auth` request body. The station MUST cross-check against `Hello.appNonce` (§4.2.2 check #2) — primary, clock-independent anti-replay defence.

issuedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this authorisation was signed.

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp after which this authorisation MUST NOT be accepted. MUST be no later than five minutes after `issuedAt`. Secondary clock-skew bound; `appNonce` (§4.2.2 check #2) is the primary anti-replay defence.
